title: Four-seven-eight breath, four rounds
subtitle: A structured breathing meditation using the 4-7-8 pattern to activate the parasympathetic nervous system before sleep.
excerpt: A short breathing meditation for Day 9 of the SLEEP program. Four rounds of the 4-7-8 pattern: four counts in, seven counts held, eight counts out. The extended exhale shifts the nervous system toward rest.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 9 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The 4-7-8 breathing technique is associated with Dr Andrew Weil, who described it as a natural tranquiliser for the nervous system.

practice.whenToUse: Use in bed as the final practice before sleep, or any time during the day when the nervous system needs a fast reset.
practice.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you have a respiratory condition that makes breath retention uncomfortable. Reduce to two rounds if you feel light-headed.

content.text: A short breathing meditation for Day 9 of the SLEEP program. The 4-7-8 pattern uses a long exhale to activate the parasympathetic nervous system — the branch responsible for rest and recovery. Four rounds takes about two minutes.
Sit or lie comfortably. Place the tip of your tongue against the ridge behind your upper front teeth and keep it there through the practice.
Exhale completely through your mouth to begin.
Inhale through your nose for 4 counts.
Exhale completely through your mouth for 8 counts.
The counts do not need to be exactly one second each — consistency within a session matters more than tempo. Let each exhale be full and audible.

Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 9 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The 4-7-8 technique is associated with Dr Andrew Weil, drawing on pranayama breathing traditions.
